skill.name: spark-optimization
skill.description: Optimize Apache Spark jobs with partitioning, caching, shuffle optimization, and memory tuning. Use when improving Spark performance, debugging slow jobs, or scaling data processing pipelines.
skill.summary: Optimize Apache Spark jobs with partitioning, caching, shuffle optimization, and memory tuning.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analyzer findings were false positives. The skill contains Python/PySpark code examples for Apache Spark optimization. External command detections misidentified Python code as Ruby/shell execution. Network findings were documentation URLs. No actual security risks detected.

content.value_statement: Slow Spark jobs waste resources and delay insights. This skill provides proven patterns for partitioning, caching, shuffle optimization, and memory tuning to accelerate your data pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Apply partitioning strategies for optimal parallelism (128MB-256MB per partition)
Implement broadcast joins for small table optimization
Configure memory settings and executor parameters
Enable Adaptive Query Execution (AQE) for automatic optimization
Detect and handle data skew with salting techniques
Optimize shuffle operations with compression and pre-aggregation
limitations: Requires Apache Spark environment (local, cluster, or cloud)
Some optimizations require Spark 3.x for AQE features
Cannot directly execute Spark jobs - provides guidance only
Configuration changes require cluster restart or new session

use_cases.title: Data Engineer Optimizing ETL Pipeline
use_cases.description: Reduce nightly batch job runtime from 4 hours to 45 minutes by implementing partition pruning, broadcast joins, and memory tuning.

use_cases.title: Analytics Team Scaling Reports
use_cases.description: Handle 10x data growth without additional infrastructure by applying AQE, proper caching strategies, and Z-ordering.

use_cases.title: Developer Debugging Slow Queries
use_cases.description: Identify and fix data skew issues causing straggler tasks using partition analysis and skew detection utilities.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Spark Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure a Spark session for a production ETL job processing 500GB of data daily. What settings should I use for partitioning and memory?
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setting up first production job

prompt_templates.title: Join Performance Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: My join between a 5TB table and 5MB lookup table is extremely slow. How can I optimize this?

prompt_templates.title: Data Skew Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: Some tasks in my groupBy operation take 10x longer than others. Help me detect and fix data skew in my Spark job.

prompt_templates.title: Full Pipeline Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Spark pipeline: read 1TB Parquet, join 3 tables, aggregate by date and region, write partitioned output. Provide complete optimization strategy including AQE, memory, and shuffle settings.

output_examples.input: Configure Spark for 100GB dataset with frequent caching
output: Recommended: Use MEMORY_AND_DISK_SER for cache persistence

output_examples.input: Fix slow join with 100x data skew
output: Solution: Apply salt join technique with 10 buckets
Alternative: Enable AQE skew join handling

best_practices: Enable Adaptive Query Execution (AQE) for automatic partition coalescing and skew handling
Use broadcast joins for tables under 50MB to eliminate shuffle overhead
Right-size partitions to 128MB-256MB for optimal parallelism without scheduling overhead
anti_patterns: Using collect() on large DataFrames causing driver OOM errors
Over-caching DataFrames that are only used once, wasting memory
Ignoring data skew which causes straggler tasks and job timeouts

faq.question: What is the optimal partition size for Spark?
faq.answer: 128MB to 256MB per partition provides the best balance between parallelism and task scheduling overhead. Too few partitions cause under-utilization; too many increase scheduling overhead.

faq.question: When should I use cache() vs persist()?
faq.answer: Use cache() for simple in-memory storage. Use persist() with specific StorageLevel when you need disk spilling (MEMORY_AND_DISK) or serialization (MEMORY_ONLY_SER) for memory efficiency.

faq.question: How do I know if my job has data skew?
faq.answer: Check Spark UI for tasks with significantly longer duration than average. A skew ratio (max/avg task time) above 2x indicates problematic skew requiring salting or AQE.

faq.question: What is Adaptive Query Execution (AQE)?
faq.answer: AQE automatically optimizes queries at runtime by coalescing partitions, handling skew joins, and optimizing aggregates. Enable with spark.sql.adaptive.enabled=true (Spark 3.0+).

faq.question: Should I use Parquet or Delta Lake?
faq.answer: Delta Lake builds on Parquet with ACID transactions, schema enforcement, and time travel. Use Delta for production workloads requiring reliability; Parquet for simple read-heavy workloads.

faq.question: How can I reduce shuffle memory pressure?
faq.answer: Enable shuffle compression (spark.shuffle.compress=true), use AQE to reduce partitions, pre-aggregate before wide transformations, and increase spark.memory.fraction if spills occur.
